test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel report showing Q4 2025 sales data by region (North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific) with monthly breakdowns and year-over-year comparison.
test_cases.expected_output: Structured Excel workbook plan with summary dashboard and data sheets

assertions.check: Output includes a Summary or Dashboard sheet as the first sheet in the workbook
Output includes units in column headers (e.g., 'Revenue ($M)', 'Growth (%)') rather than bare column names
Output includes a totals row at the bottom of numerical data or explicitly mentions summing/aggregating values
Output describes a meaningful data sort order (by date, region, or value) rather than leaving data unsorted
Output does NOT use placeholder text like 'data here', 'TBD', or 'insert values' in the actual data cells

test_cases.prompt: Write a professional memo to the engineering team about the new code review policy taking effect April 1, 2026.
test_cases.expected_output: Structured Word document following memo format standards

assertions.check: Output starts with a heading level 1 for the document title or memo subject
Output does NOT place two headings back-to-back without content paragraphs between them
Output uses predominantly active voice ('The team will implement' rather than 'The policy will be implemented by the team')
Output front-loads the most important information (the policy change and effective date) in the opening paragraph or summary
If the output includes tables, they have descriptive headers (not 'Column A', 'Column B') or the output contains no tables at all

test_cases.prompt: Create a PowerPoint presentation for an investor pitch about a mobile health app startup. Include 8 slides covering problem, solution, market, business model, traction, team, financials, and ask.
test_cases.expected_output: Structured presentation with title slide, body slides, and CTA

assertions.check: Output specifies the first slide as a title layout with the presentation title and context
Output follows the 6x6 rule (max 6 bullets per slide, max 6 words per bullet) or explicitly limits bullet content per slide
Each slide focuses on a single topic (problem, solution, market, etc.) rather than combining multiple topics on one slide
Output ends with a clear call-to-action, ask amount, or next steps slide
Output does NOT contain 'Lorem ipsum', placeholder body text, or 'Your text here' in any slide content

test_cases.prompt: Create a PDF invoice for a freelance web development project. Client: Acme Corp. Total: $12,500. Include itemized services, payment terms, and contact info.
test_cases.expected_output: Structured PDF with heading/paragraph pairs, proper formatting

assertions.check: Output structures the PDF as heading/paragraph pairs with content between each heading
Output formats currency values with proper notation (e.g., $12,500.00 with commas and decimal places)
Output includes an itemized breakdown of services with individual costs rather than just a single total
Paragraphs in the output are kept concise (not exceeding approximately 100 words each for PDF readability)
Output uses descriptive section titles (e.g., 'Services Rendered', 'Payment Terms') rather than generic labels like 'Section 1'

test_cases.prompt: Create a multi-sheet Excel workbook tracking a 12-week project plan with tasks, owners, status, and a Gantt-style timeline view.
test_cases.expected_output: Multi-sheet Excel workbook with project tracking and visual timeline

assertions.check: Output describes multiple sheets or tabs in the workbook (not everything on a single sheet)
Output uses descriptive column headers like 'Task Name', 'Owner', 'Status', 'Start Date' rather than 'A', 'B', 'C'
Output specifies date formatting (ISO 8601 in data or localized in display) for timeline/date columns
Output defines specific status categories or values (e.g., 'Not Started', 'In Progress', 'Complete', 'Blocked') rather than freeform text
Output maintains a logical reading order with summary or dashboard information accessible before detailed data
